text.title: SOC 2 Evidence Generator CLI Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every SOC 2 Evidence Generator CLI application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Each merged PR MUST emit exactly one evidence stub. Multiple stubs per PR (e.g. one per commit) are rejected as noise; zero stubs are rejected as gap.
rule.rationale: PR-as-unit aligns with the change-management boundary auditors already sample; commit-level noise overwhelms the store; missing stubs are audit findings.

rule.statement: Every stub MUST be cryptographically signed at write time with a key in the evidence-signing keyring. Unsigned stubs are rejected at the store ingress.
rule.rationale: Unsigned evidence can be forged after the fact; signing is the auditor-acceptable tamper-evidence layer.

rule.statement: Every stub MUST carry >=1 control label from the engagement's control vocabulary. Stubs without labels are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without labels, the stub cannot be queried by control during an audit; the unlabeled stub is functionally invisible.

rule.statement: Stubs MUST embed the PR approval link (GitHub/GitLab review URL) + reviewer identity. Stubs without approval evidence are rejected.
rule.rationale: The approver is the human attestation; SOC 2 CC8.1 / CC3.2 cares about that signature specifically.

rule.statement: Stubs land in an append-only, retention-protected store (S3 Object Lock, immutable bucket, write-once table). Mutable storage is rejected.
rule.rationale: Auditors require evidence-store immutability to accept stubs as evidence rather than 'developer notes'.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
